heading: Hospital protocols for organ procurement and standards for organ procurement agencies

Existing Agreements With Organ Procurement Agencies, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–432, title I, § 155(a)(2)108 Stat. 4439Oct. 31, 1994“Any hospital or rural primary care hospital which has an agreement (as defined in section 1138(a)(3)(A) of the Social Security Act []) with an organ procurement agency other than such hospital’s designated organ procurement agency (as defined in section 1138(a)(3)(B) of such Act) on the date of the enactment of this section [] shall, if such hospital desires to continue such agreement on and after the effective date of the amendments made by paragraph (1) [see Effective Date of 1994 Amendment note above], submit an application to the Secretary for a waiver under section 1138(a)(2) of such Act not later than , and such agreement may continue in effect pending the Secretary’s determination with respect to such application.”42 U.S.C. 1320b–8(a)(3)(A)Oct. 31, 1994January 1, 1996

L. 103–432, title I, § 155(a)(2)108 Stat. 4439Oct. 31, 1994“Any hospital or rural primary care hospital which has an agreement (as defined in section 1138(a)(3)(A) of the Social Security Act []) with an organ procurement agency other than such hospital’s designated organ procurement agency (as defined in section 1138(a)(3)(B) of such Act) on the date of the enactment of this section [] shall, if such hospital desires to continue such agreement on and after the effective date of the amendments made by paragraph (1) [see Effective Date of 1994 Amendment note above], submit an application to the Secretary for a waiver under section 1138(a)(2) of such Act not later than , and such agreement may continue in effect pending the Secretary’s determination with respect to such application.”42 U.S.C. 1320b–8(a)(3)(A)Oct. 31, 1994January 1, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: assure that families of potential organ donors are made aware of the option of organ or tissue donation and their option to decline,

children.label: ii
children.text: encourage discretion and sensitivity with respect to the circumstances, views, and beliefs of such families, and

children.label: iii
children.text: require that such hospital’s designated organ procurement agency (as defined in paragraph (3)(B)) is notified of potential organ donors;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a hospital in which organ transplants are performed, the hospital is a member of, and abides by the rules and requirements of, the Organ Procurement and Transplantation Network established pursuant to (in this section referred to as the “Network”); andsection 274 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the hospital or critical access hospital has an agreement (as defined in paragraph (3)(A)) only with such hospital’s designated organ procurement agency.

children.label: i
children.text: the waiver is expected to increase organ donation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the waiver will assure equitable treatment of patients referred for transplants within the service area served by such hospital’s designated organ procurement agency and within the service area served by the organ procurement agency with which the hospital seeks to enter into an agreement under the waiver.

children.label: i
children.text: cost effectiveness;

children.label: ii
children.text: improvements in quality;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether there has been any change in a hospital’s designated organ procurement agency due to a change made on or after , in the definitions for metropolitan statistical areas (as established by the Office of Management and Budget); andDecember 28, 1992

children.label: iv
children.text: the length and continuity of a hospital’s relationship with an organ procurement agency other than the hospital’s designated organ procurement agency;

children.label: C
children.text: Any hospital or critical access hospital seeking a waiver under subparagraph (A) shall submit an application to the Secretary containing such information as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: publish a public notice of any waiver application received from a hospital or critical access hospital under this paragraph within 30 days of receiving such application; and

children.label: ii
children.text: prior to making a final determination on such application under subparagraph (A), offer interested parties the opportunity to submit written comments to the Secretary during the 60-day period beginning on the date such notice is published.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “agreement” means an agreement described in ;section 273(b)(3)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the term “designated organ procurement agency” means, with respect to a hospital or critical access hospital, the organ procurement agency designated pursuant to subsection (b) for the service area in which such hospital is located; and

children.label: C
children.text: the term “organ” means a human kidney, liver, heart, lung, pancreas, and any other human organ or tissue specified by the Secretary for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: is a qualified organ procurement organization (as described in ) that is operating under a grant made under , or (ii) has been certified or recertified by the Secretary within the previous 2 years (4 years if the Secretary determines appropriate for an organization on the basis of its past practices) as meeting the standards to be a qualified organ procurement organization (as so described);section 273(b) of this titlesection 273(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: meets the requirements that are applicable under such subchapter for organ procurement agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: meets performance-related standards prescribed by the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: is a member of, and abides by the rules and requirements of, the Network;

children.label: E
children.text: allocates organs, within its service area and nationally, in accordance with medical criteria and the policies of the Network; and

children.label: F
children.text: is designated by the Secretary as an organ procurement organization payments to which may be treated as organ procurement costs for purposes of reimbursement under such subchapter.

The Secretary may not designate more than one organ procurement organization for each service area (described in section 273(b)(1)(E) of this title) under paragraph (1)(F).1See References in Text note below.1
